extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters by direction and category: I counted only the “Against” rows—criminal proceedings were Nil for the Company, Directors, Subsidiaries and KMP/Senior Management (0); statutory/regulatory actions were Nil for the Company and Directors, 8 against Subsidiaries, and Nil against KMP/Senior Management (8); tax proceedings were 4 against the Company plus 10 against Subsidiaries (14). I excluded all “By” rows, including the Company’s 4 tax matters and Subsidiaries’ 2 criminal, 11 tax and 1 civil matters. The contingent-liabilities figure is the stated consolidated Total of ₹4,941.44 million as of March 31, 2025, converted to ₹494.144 crore; the litigation aggregate against the Company and Subsidiaries is ₹34.07 million + ₹1,237.62 million = ₹1,271.69 million, converted to ₹127.169 crore. The source tables use ₹ in million, so the conversion is million ÷ 10 to crore. The only going-concern uncertainty discussed relates to subsidiary LPRL, while the statutory auditor’s opinion was expressly not modified; no company-wide top-five or top-ten customer concentration percentage is disclosed.
